author: Tayyab Shinwari
text:
Most students study everything.

Top performers identify the 20% that drives 80% of the results.

Here's why:

The Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule) suggests that a small number of actions often produce the majority of outcomes.

In learning, that means:

• A few key concepts drive most exam questions

• A handful of skills create most academic success

• A small number of study habits produce the biggest gains
Yet most students do the opposite.

They spend hours:

❌ Highlighting every page

❌ Treating every topic as equally important

❌ Trying to memorize everything

The highest achievers focus differently.

They ask:

✓ Which concepts appear most often?

✓ Which mistakes cost me the most marks?

✓ Which skills have the biggest impact on performance?

✓ What's the smallest effort that creates the largest improvement?

The goal isn't to learn less.

It's to learn what matters most.

Because studying harder can make you busy.

Studying smarter makes you effective.

The biggest mistake students make is believing:

"Everything is important."

The reality?

Not all effort creates equal results.

Find the critical 20%.

Master it.

And watch your results multiply.

📌 Remember:

20% of what you study often produces 80% of your results.

The question is:

Are you focusing on the vital few or the trivial many?

#ParetoPrinciple #8020Rule #LearningScience #StudyTips #Productivity #Education #StudentSuccess #Learning #SelfImprovement #GrowthMindset

author: Carolina Erices Garcia
text:
🎂Hoy cumplí 55 años. Más arrugas. Más canas, pero más rica que nunca.

Me miro al espejo y veo…

Un cuerpo con 15 kilos que sobran.
Canas que tapo cada dos semanas.
Y marcas en mi piel que va dejando el paso de los años.

Y aún así, me siento millonaria.

Por una parte cuento con una familia preciosa. 

Mi hija se casa este año, mi papá volvió a mi vida.

Cuento con un compañero que en apaña en todas, mi hija postiza tendrá su tercera niñita y lo más importante: todos sanos.

Por otro lado en lo profesional…

Nunca en mi vida había crecido tanto y haciendo algo que me apasiona.

No tengo un MBA, ni salí de la mejor Universidad de Chile, sin embargo desarrollé una profesión que no existe en ninguna universidad: ser Consultora en LinkedIn y Social Selling.

→ Realizando Mentorías 1:1 de Posicionamiento de Marca
→ Gestionando cuentas de CEOs y fundadores
→ Charlas y Talleres como Speaker
→ Capacitando Equipos Comerciales a vender más por LinkedIn

Con ingresos que me permiten ahorrar y vivir tranquila.

Esta carrera no nace con ningún título.
Nace de estudio continuo, de experiencia, de práctica, y mucho mucho trabajo.
La cual construí en base a pura convicción.

El espejo me muestra una versión con más años.
Pero mi vida me muestra una versión que nunca había tenido.

A los 55, las arrugas cuentan historias.
Las canas son medallas.
Y la riqueza no se mide en lo que refleja el espejo.

Se mide en lo que te hace latir el corazón.

¿Has pensado cuál es tu riqueza que ves en el espejo?

author: Confidencial
text:
Cape Verde is one of the smallest nations ever to qualify for a World Cup. On June 15, 2026, in their first ever World Cup match, they held Spain to a 0-0 draw.

The man who made it possible is Vozinha. 

He is 40 years old. 

He plays for Chaves in Portugal's second division. 

His market value before the match was 40,000.

He made seven saves against the second ranked team in the world. 

He won Man of the Match.

Before the game he had 48,000 Instagram followers. Within hours of the final whistle he had nearly 5 million.

After the match he broke down in tears. 

His mother could not get a visa to be there. 

His grandparents, who raised him, passed away before this moment.

One performance on the world's biggest stage changed everything.

The lesson is simple. 

You do not need the biggest resources, the biggest team, or the biggest name. 

You need to be ready when the moment comes. 

And when it does, you have to show up completely.

author: Foram A.
text:
Even the trusted partner of every organization nowadays — AI — is not fully agreeing with some of the decisions being made.

I asked AI, 'Why is it taking people's jobs?' 
— and this was its response.

I'm not taking anyone's job.

I'm doing exactly what a calculator did to manual calculations, what Excel did to ledgers, and what email did to postal memos.

I can help with:
Drafting Summary
Organizing information
Generating ideas
Automating repetitive work

But I don't:
Take responsibility for decisions
Understand office politics
Build trust with clients
Negotiate salaries
Read emotions perfectly
Convince a candidate to relocate after three offer dropouts 😭

As a recruiter, you know this better than most people.

I cannot build the relationships, judgment, market understanding, persuasion skills, and intuition that make a person successful.

The real danger isn't AI replacing people.
It's ORGANIZATIONS ASSUMING that because a task can be automated, the human behind that task is no longer valuable.

Those are two very different things.

In fact, the reason you and I have had so many discussions about recruitment, corporate culture, and hiring is because those topics require human experiences and opinions. I can help shape the words, but the observations come from people like you who are actually living them.

I am not taking anyone's job.

People who think an entire work can simply be replaced by AI are the ones creating the problem.

I'm just a robot at the end of the day.

#AI #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureOfWork #Technology #CorporateLife #Innovation #HumanIntelligence

author: Pathway Franchising
text:
Let’s talk about Side Hustle! I am asked about franchising as a side hustle every week. There are ama inc opportunities to start alternate income streams, additional profits, and diversify your wealth.  Firstly not every franchise is Side Hustle appropriate. So that’s where a broker can help. 

There are a few things you may have to do.
Here are some considerations: 

- Franchises don’t run themselves : Most likely you need a strong manager 
- Investments need oversight : You will probably need to spend at least 15 hours in the business every week.
- Funding : You may need more funds if you run your business semi-absent.  Managers are an additional expense.
- Time : Consider the time to break even and earn a profit. Compare your side hustle to jumping in full time.

Want to learn more ? info@pathwayfranchising.com

#franchising #pathwayfranchising #entrepreneur #sidehustle

author: Carlos Peña López
text:
Una de las peores prisiones es estar en un trabajo que detestas y por la razón que sea no poder renunciar.

Bueno, aquí en esta película se ve mucho de eso. Estar atascado en una situación laboral donde no existe escapatoria y pese a todo tener que seguir. Las razones son variadas: carecer de competencias para moverse a otro lugar mejor, tener una edad en que se cierra el mercado, las deudas que te atornillan a tu posición actual, una economía deprimida que hunde al mercado y podríamos seguir.

El mayor problema es cuando el empleador reconoce esa debilidad y con mala leche te explota. Te sabe acorralado y en ese rincón te golpea con toda alevosía abusando de tu vulnerabilidad.

No diré más.

Esta película sorprende y un spoiler sería un pecado imperdonable.

#trabajo #empatía #saludmental

author: Fernando Zamorano Inostroza
text:
𝗟𝗮𝘀 𝗳𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘀 𝗮 𝗘𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘂𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗮 𝗱𝗲𝗹 𝗔𝗻̃𝗼 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲 𝘆𝗮 𝗳𝘂𝗲𝗿𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗱𝗮𝘀

Al revisar la lista de finalistas de Ejecutiva del Año 2026, resulta difícil no notar la diversidad de industrias y empresas representadas.

Durante años hablamos de aumentar la participación femenina en posiciones de liderazgo.

Hoy la conversación ya no parece centrarse en si pueden llegar a estos cargos, sino en cómo están liderando organizaciones cada vez más complejas, tecnológicas y exigentes.

En esta selección aparecen ejecutivas de sectores tan diversos como minería, retail, telecomunicaciones, salud, seguros, logística y consumo masivo.

Más allá de quién resulte ganadora, creo que es una señal positiva ver a tantas mujeres ocupando posiciones estratégicas en algunas de las organizaciones más relevantes del país.

El liderazgo sigue evolucionando, y listas como esta muestran que el talento, la visión y la capacidad de ejecución están presentes en todos los sectores.

¿Cuál creen que es el principal desafío que enfrentan hoy quienes lideran grandes organizaciones?

Fuente: Diario Financiero

author: Saúl Jacob Vergara T.
text:
💭 Everyone talks about disrupting industries.

But what if we disrupted something even bigger?

What if we disrupted the way we treat each other?

In a world obsessed with speed, competition, and constant change, the most powerful transformation may come from something surprisingly simple:

Being more human.


If you're interested in fashion and want to check out stylish outfit ideas, read this blog 👇👇👇

20 Old Money Summer Outfits That Look Expensive Without Breaking the Bank👇👇👇
https://lnkd.in/dA3jYq4b?



📜 8 simple lessons that can change workplaces, communities, and lives:

1️⃣ Kindness is stronger than being right.
The next time a conversation gets heated, listen before you respond. Understanding creates more progress than winning arguments ever will.

2️⃣ The walls between us are often imaginary.
A smile. A sincere "How are you?" A moment of genuine interest. Human connection remains our greatest superpower.

3️⃣ We have more in common than we realize.
Different backgrounds. Different stories. Yet the same hopes, fears, dreams, and desire to belong.

4️⃣ Everyone is carrying something you cannot see.
The colleague who seems distracted. The customer who appears impatient. The teammate who feels difficult. Lead with compassion—you rarely know the full story.

5️⃣ Every small action shapes the future.
Support local businesses. Mentor someone starting their journey. Offer encouragement. Let someone merge into traffic. Small choices create powerful ripple effects.

6️⃣ Peace isn't the absence of conflict.
It's choosing respect, empathy, and understanding in the middle of disagreement.

7️⃣ Shared joy grows. Shared pain shrinks.
Celebrate people's victories. Stand beside them in difficult moments. That's how trust and lasting relationships are built.

8️⃣ Change begins with the person in the mirror.
Your attitude, energy, and actions influence everyone around you. Be the example you wish existed more often.

🌍 My vision:

A workplace where curiosity replaces judgment.
Where diversity fuels innovation.
Where people feel seen, valued, and respected.
Where kindness isn't extraordinary...

It's simply how we do things.

✨ Because the world changes when ordinary people choose extraordinary compassion.

👇 What is one small act of kindness or positive change you're committing to today?

#Leadership #Kindness #WorkplaceCulture #Humanity #Empathy #DiversityAndInclusion #PersonalGrowth #Teamwork #Networking #FutureOfWork #PositiveLeadership #CultureMatters #Inspiration #Mindset #Togetherness

author: Domingo Boscan - Sup. Estructuras - Ing. Civil Mecanico - Universidad de Chile
text:
🚊 Hoy hablaremos sobre el escantillón de la vía, también conocido como ancho de vía.

🚂 Básicamente, el escantillón es la distancia que existe entre los dos rieles del tren, medida desde sus caras internas. En este caso, hablamos de 1435 milímetros, o 1.435 metros, que corresponde al llamado ancho estándar internacional.

🚆 Este ancho es muy importante porque es el más utilizado en el mundo. De hecho, más de la mitad de las vías ferroviarias a nivel mundial están construidas con esta medida.

🚊 ¿Por qué se usa tanto?
Primero, porque permite que trenes, locomotoras y vagones puedan operar en diferentes redes ferroviarias sin necesidad de modificaciones, lo que facilita la conexión entre regiones o incluso países.

🚆 Además, este ancho proporciona mayor estabilidad al tren, algo fundamental cuando circula en curvas o a altas velocidades. Por eso, muchos trenes modernos y de alta velocidad utilizan este estándar.
